key_insight: Scaling up GPT-style language models delays but does not eliminate plasticity loss, and the onset appears to grow sublinearly with model size.
engineering_relevance: For continual-learning NLP systems, simply increasing parameter count is not enough to guarantee long-term adaptability; training strategy and mitigation methods still matter.
method: Trained GPT-style Transformer models from 5M to 314M non-embedding parameters
Used a multilingual continual-learning setup to probe adaptation over time
Also tested stationary multilingual training to check whether plasticity loss requires abrupt task switches
evidence_signal: Plasticity loss was measured by worsening performance on a held-out Vietnamese probing task, with deterioration observed across the model sizes studied.
watch_item: The reported scaling trend delays measurable plasticity loss, but it does not prove that larger models are immune or that the effect disappears in real deployments.
paper_title: Can Scale Save Us From Plasticity Loss in Large Language Models?
